name: Chocolate Mousse Torte
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Maida Heatter cookbooks.
Adjust rack to center of oven. Preheat oven to 350°F. Butter a 9-inch ovenproof glass pie pan. Dust it with fine dry bread crumbs, set aside.
Place chocolate in the top of a small double boiler over hot water. Dissolve the coffee in the boiling water and pour it over the chocolate. Cover and let stand over low heat, stirring occasionally with a small wire whisk, until chocolate is almost melted. Remove chocolate from the heat and continue to stir until smooth. Set aside to cool slightly.
In the bowl of an electric mixer, beat egg yolks at high speed for about 5 minutes until they are pale lemon colored and thickened. Gradually add sugar and continue to beat at high speed for another 5 minutes until very thick. Add vanilla and chocolate, beating slowly and scraping the bowl with a rubber spatula until well blended. Remove from mixer.
In the large bowl of an electric mixer, beat egg whites with salt until stiff but not dry. Gradually fold in about half of the whites into the chocolate mixture, then fold the remaining chocolate into the remaining whites, folding only until no whites show. Handle as little as possible.
Gently remove and set aside about 4 cups of the mousse. Turn the balance into the pie plate; it will barely reach the top. Very gently spread to a level surface and place in the oven to bake. Cover the reserved mousse and refrigerate.
Bake the mousse for 25 minutes, then turn off the heat and let sit in the oven for an additional 5 minutes. Remove from the heat and cool on a rack. The mousse will rise during baking and fall while cooling, settling in the center and leaving a high rim. When completely cool, remove reserved mousse from the refrigerator and place the refrigerated mousse into the center of the baked mousse shell.
Handle as little as possible or it will lose the air beaten into it. Optional: I used sugar cocoa and instant coffee to flavor the whipped cream; flavored to taste. The whipped cream is optional but looks better with it.
